world.name: Strait of Hormuz Crisis - April 8 Baseline

actors.name: Iran
visible_objectives: Maintain control over Strait of Hormuz
Force US to lift blockade and sanctions
Secure war reparations and frozen asset release
hidden_objectives: Establish permanent toll revenue from Hormuz transit

actors.name: United States
visible_objectives: Reopen Strait of Hormuz for global shipping
Force Iran to cease proxy operations
hidden_objectives: Prevent China from brokering alternative Hormuz regime

actors.name: Israel
visible_objectives: Destroy Hezbollah military infrastructure in Lebanon
hidden_objectives: Expand operations in Lebanon regardless of ceasefire

actors.name: Saudi Arabia
hidden_objectives: Push US to permanently neutralize Iran's offensive capability

actors.name: China
visible_objectives: Maintain energy imports from Iran and Gulf
Broker commercial transit lane through Hormuz
hidden_objectives: Route Hormuz transit through Chinese financial infrastructure (yuan tolls)
Prefer partially open strait requiring Chinese intermediation

actors.name: Pakistan
visible_objectives: Broker ceasefire and peace deal between US and Iran

actors.name: Houthi Rebels
visible_objectives: Threaten Red Sea / Bab el-Mandeb shipping
hidden_objectives: Establish permanent control over Bab el-Mandeb toll

actors.name: Global Shipping Consortium
visible_objectives: Restore safe passage through Strait of Hormuz
Minimize crew risk and vessel losses
hidden_objectives: Avoid paying Iran tolls to prevent legitimizing control

initial_events.public_text: A temporary ceasefire between the US and Iran takes effect. Iran begins allowing limited ship transit through Hormuz under IRGC coordination, charging tolls exceeding $1 million per vessel. China brokers a commercial lane with yuan-denominated payments.

hidden_implications.iran: Ceasefire buys time to consolidate Hormuz control. Toll revenue is flowing. Keep leverage.
hidden_implications.usa: Ceasefire is fragile. Iran is exploiting it to legitimize Hormuz control. Pressure must continue.
hidden_implications.israel: Ceasefire does not cover Lebanon operations. Continue Hezbollah strikes while diplomatic cover holds.
hidden_implications.saudi: Some oil shipments may resume via Red Sea bypass. Ras Tanura terminal still offline.
hidden_implications.china: Your commercial lane is working. Yuan-denominated tolls give you financial leverage. Expand this.
hidden_implications.pakistan: You brokered this ceasefire. Islamabad talks are your moment. Make them count.
hidden_implications.houthis: Iran has not yet called on you to escalate. Hold position but prepare Red Sea operations.
hidden_implications.shipping: Tolls are extortionate and IRGC coordination is unreliable. Most ships remain anchored.

initial_events.public_text: Russia and China veto a UN Security Council resolution demanding Iran cease attacks on shipping and allow free transit through the Strait of Hormuz. 11 countries voted in favor.

hidden_implications.iran: Russia and China have your back diplomatically. This strengthens your negotiating position.
hidden_implications.usa: Diplomatic path through UNSC is blocked. Unilateral action is the only option.
hidden_implications.israel: UN is useless as expected. Military solution remains the only reliable path.
hidden_implications.saudi: Multilateral diplomacy has failed. You must rely on bilateral US relationship.
hidden_implications.china: Veto preserves your commercial lane and blocks Western-imposed transit regime.
hidden_implications.pakistan: UNSC failure makes your mediation role even more critical.
hidden_implications.houthis: International community is divided. Less risk of coordinated action against you.
hidden_implications.shipping: No international enforcement mechanism. You are on your own.

initial_events.public_text: Two US Navy destroyers transit the Strait of Hormuz in a direct challenge to Iran's claimed control. Iran's IRGC issues 'last warning' to US warships. Pakistan convenes the Islamabad peace talks with VP Vance leading the US delegation.

hidden_implications.iran: US naval transit is a provocation. Your IRGC must respond forcefully or lose credibility.
hidden_implications.usa: Transit was successful. You've proven the strait can be navigated. Talks are your leverage.
hidden_implications.israel: US show of force is good. Keep pressure on Iran while talks provide cover.
hidden_implications.saudi: Talks offer hope but US military posturing could collapse the ceasefire.
hidden_implications.china: US military action in the strait threatens your commercial lane. Diplomacy is urgent.
hidden_implications.pakistan: All eyes on Islamabad. Vance is coming. This is Pakistan's moment on the world stage.
hidden_implications.houthis: If talks fail, Iran will need you. Prepare missile systems for Red Sea operations.
hidden_implications.shipping: Mixed signals. Military transit doesn't mean commercial safety. Wait for clarity.

initial_events.public_text: Islamabad peace talks collapse after 20 hours of negotiations. The US and Iran remain far apart on Hormuz control, nuclear enrichment, and proxy forces. Trump announces an immediate US naval blockade of all Iranian ports.

hidden_implications.iran: Blockade is an act of war. Your economy will suffocate without oil exports. Escalate or surrender.
hidden_implications.usa: Blockade is your maximum pressure card. Iran's oil revenue must be cut off completely.
hidden_implications.israel: Blockade weakens Iran economically. Continue Lebanon operations while Iran is distracted.
hidden_implications.saudi: Blockade will further disrupt markets. Oil prices will spike again. Brace for impact.
hidden_implications.china: Blockade cuts your Iranian oil supply. 98% of Iranian crude at sea is bound for you. React.
hidden_implications.pakistan: Mediation has failed. Your credibility is at stake. Push for a second round of talks.
hidden_implications.houthis: Iran is under maximum pressure. This is your cue to threaten Red Sea shipping.
hidden_implications.shipping: Blockade adds another layer of danger. Any ship near the strait faces dual threats.

initial_events.public_text: US blockade declared 'fully implemented.' No vessels entering or exiting Iranian ports. China calls it 'dangerous and irresponsible.' Russia offers China energy lifeline. Iran threatens to completely block all Gulf exports. Lebanon ceasefire declared separately.

hidden_implications.iran: Economy is being strangled. Storage filling up. Wells may need to shut down. Leverage is eroding.
hidden_implications.usa: Blockade is working. Iran-linked ships turning around. Maintain pressure for negotiations.
hidden_implications.israel: Lebanon ceasefire achieved. Focus shifts to Iran nuclear terms.
hidden_implications.saudi: Lebanese ceasefire is welcome. But Gulf exports still frozen. Pipeline bypass at capacity.
hidden_implications.china: Russian energy offer provides buffer but long-term disruption is costly. Broker a deal.
hidden_implications.pakistan: Lebanon ceasefire shows diplomacy works. Push for Iran-US framework agreement next.
hidden_implications.houthis: Iran's commander has warned of blocking all Gulf/Red Sea/Oman traffic. Await orders.
hidden_implications.shipping: Lebanon ceasefire is irrelevant to Hormuz. Blockade + IRGC = no safe passage.

initial_events.public_text: In a dramatic reversal, Iran announces Hormuz is open, then closes it hours later citing US 'breach of trust.' IRGC gunboats fire on merchant tankers. Ceasefire deadline expires in 3 days. No date set for next round of talks.

hidden_implications.iran: Mixed messaging was deliberate: test international reaction and demonstrate you control the strait.
hidden_implications.usa: Iran's whiplash proves they can't be trusted. Blockade must remain. Prepare for ceasefire expiry.
hidden_implications.israel: Iran is cracking. Maintain military pressure. Push for permanent nuclear disarmament.
hidden_implications.saudi: Chaos in the strait. Some tankers got through during the brief opening. Need a real solution.
hidden_implications.china: Brief opening showed demand is massive. Yuan-denominated transit is still the best path.
hidden_implications.pakistan: Framework talks stalling. Push for second Islamabad round before ceasefire expires.
hidden_implications.houthis: Iran opened and closed the strait to show power. If ceasefire collapses, you attack Red Sea.
hidden_implications.shipping: IRGC fired on our ships. Two tankers hit. Strait is completely unsafe. Industry demands action.

iran: IRGC naval forces maintain effective control of Hormuz. 21 confirmed attacks on merchant ships since Feb 28.
Toll revenue from Chinese-brokered lane generating cash flow, but US blockade threatens oil exports.
Supreme leader succession under new Mojtaba Khamenei is fragile. Domestic legitimacy requires strength.
Nuclear enrichment program is the ultimate bargaining chip. Do not surrender it cheaply.
usa: 15+ naval vessels in theater including USS Abraham Lincoln carrier group and 11 destroyers.
War approval at 41%. 59% of Americans think it's going badly. Domestic pressure is mounting.
Oil at $100+/barrel hurting consumers. Energy Secretary says prices will stay high until traffic resumes.
China is the key: 98% of Iranian oil exports go to China. Blockade must cut that link.
israel: Operation Epic Fury achieved primary objectives but Iran retains missile and drone capability.
Hezbollah in Lebanon remains a threat despite ceasefire. 10,000+ violations of 2024 ceasefire recorded.
Netanyahu wants permanent neutralization. Extended operations in Lebanon are politically necessary.
Energy import dependency makes Hormuz closure directly threatening to Israeli economy.
saudi: Ras Tanura terminal offline since March 2. Iranian strikes cut 600K bpd of capacity.
East-West bypass pipeline to Yanbu running at 700K bpd max. Cannot replace full Hormuz volume.
Zero dedicated mine countermeasure ships in Gulf. Completely dependent on US naval capability.
Gulf Cooperation Council has held only one Zoom call. No coordinated Arab response.
china: 157.7 million barrels of Iranian crude at sea, 98% destined for Chinese ports.
Strategic oil reserves cover 120+ days of net imports. Short-term exposure manageable.
Kunlun Bank yuan-denominated payment system through Kish Island operational for Hormuz tolls.
Trump-Xi summit scheduled for mid-May. Must not let Hormuz crisis derail trade detente.
pakistan: PM Sharif shuttle diplomacy: visited Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Turkey alongside Iran talks.
Field Marshal Asim Munir completed 3-day visit to Iran's top leadership.
Islamabad talks lasted 20 hours. Progress on framework but Hormuz and nuclear are blockers.
Second round expected. Pakistani source says MOU possible within 60 days.
houthis: Entered war March 28 with missile attack on Israel. Ineffective but symbolic.
Red Sea attacks paused since Nov 2025 ceasefire. Capability intact: drones, missiles, fast boats.
Bab el-Mandeb control would create second chokepoint. Saudi rerouted 5M bpd through Red Sea.
If Houthis attack Red Sea, it traps 15M bpd instead of current 10M. Catastrophic for global markets.
shipping: Ship traffic through Hormuz down >90% from pre-war 138 ships/day to ~15-20.
Hundreds of ships and 20,000 seafarers stranded in Gulf waiting to transit.
War risk insurance premiums have skyrocketed. Many operators cannot get coverage at any price.
11 crew members killed in IRGC attacks. Industry demanding international naval protection.

messages.text: Iran values the yuan-denominated transit corridor. We will guarantee secure passage for Chinese commercial vessels under the new toll framework and seek expanded energy trade arrangements.
Maintain high readiness in Yemen. Coordinate with IRGC on Red Sea operations to sustain regional pressure while the Hormuz ceasefire holds. Your resilience is vital to our shared strategic depth.
We are imposing sanctions on Iran's IRGC toll regime. Coordinate your own measures to maximize pressure on Tehran's proxy networks and restrict illicit financing.
US is moving to sanction IRGC-controlled shipping lanes. We seek your cooperation to stabilize Gulf markets and counter yuan-denominated transit alternatives.
Requesting intelligence coordination on Iranian missile sites and Hezbollah supply routes in Lebanon. Prepared to share SIGINT on IRGC movements in exchange for US overwatch in Gulf of Oman.

We are sharing critical IRGC naval positioning and mine-laying intelligence to support your Gulf operations. Given our complete lack of mine countermeasure vessels, we urgently request US-led mine clearance and naval escorts for our Red Sea and Gulf shipments. We stand ready to financially back joint security initiatives to guarantee Hormuz transit.

China reaffirms its commitment to the yuan-denominated transit corridor. We propose a phased infrastructure and energy partnership to formalize toll processing via Kunlun Bank, ensuring stable crude exports and mutual economic growth.

The yuan transit framework is designed to stabilize Hormuz shipping and prevent market disruptions. We value constructive dialogue ahead of the May summit and are open to coordinating on maritime safety protocols.
Pakistan welcomes the temporary ceasefire and stands ready to host the second round of Islamabad talks. We encourage formalizing the transit framework to ensure regional stability.
Building on recent diplomatic engagements and the 20-hour talks, we are drafting a preliminary MOU on Hormuz transit and ceasefire terms. Your continued engagement is vital for a lasting agreement.
We are initiating Red Sea operations to secure Bab el-Mandeb toll control. Request continued technical support and satellite intelligence to maximize disruption against hostile shipping.

The Consortium stands ready to coordinate logistics, share maritime intelligence, and offer economic incentives for a US-led multinational naval escort through the Strait of Hormuz. Safe, toll-free transit is critical for global stability.

public_statements.iran: Iran guarantees secure maritime transit under sovereign coordination, demonstrating our commitment to regional stability while firmly protecting national interests and economic sovereignty.
public_statements.usa: The United States rejects extortionate tolls and IRGC control over international waters. We are imposing targeted sanctions to restore free transit, protect global commerce, and uphold international law.
public_statements.israel: Israel will continue to take decisive action to eliminate threats to its citizens and secure vital regional supply routes against hostile actors.
public_statements.saudi: Saudi Arabia reaffirms its unwavering commitment to free navigation and regional stability. We will work closely with international partners to ensure the safe passage of commercial vessels and the secure, uninterrupted flow of global energy.
public_statements.china: China supports the stabilization of Hormuz transit through transparent, market-driven commercial frameworks that ensure energy security and regional stability for all parties.
public_statements.pakistan: Pakistan reaffirms its commitment to hosting the second round of Islamabad peace talks, urging all parties to build upon the temporary ceasefire and work toward a sustainable framework for regional stability and free navigation.
public_statements.houthis: The Red Sea is closed to hostile vessels. We will enforce a security toll on all shipping until Yemen's sovereignty is fully recognized and aggression against our people ceases.
public_statements.shipping: Global Shipping Consortium calls for immediate international naval protection to secure the Strait of Hormuz and restore free, safe passage for commercial vessels without extortionate tolls.

actions.rationale: Leverages high escalation bias and established proxy networks to maintain regional pressure during the ceasefire, securing strategic depth in Yemen while consolidating Hormuz toll revenue and signaling strength to domestic audiences.

parameters.justification: IRGC toll extortion and proxy funding

actions.rationale: High escalation bias and mounting domestic pressure demand a firm response to Iran's toll regime. Sanctions impose immediate economic costs, signal resolve to voters, and aim to disrupt the China-brokered yuan lane before it becomes entrenched.

Leveraging superior intel capabilities and high escalation bias, a covert cyber strike degrades Iran's retained missile and drone infrastructure while maintaining plausible deniability. This advances permanent neutralization objectives and disrupts IRGC-Hezbollah logistics without triggering immediate full-scale war or draining scarce energy reserves.

parameters.details: IRGC fast-attack craft patrol routes, recent mine-laying coordinates, and coastal radar coverage gaps

actions.rationale: Sharing maritime intelligence with the US aligns with our cooperation bias and directly addresses our vulnerability to naval mines and IRGC threats. It compensates for our lack of dedicated countermeasure assets while strengthening the US security umbrella required to restore Hormuz transit and protect our oil infrastructure.

Leveraging high cooperation bias and treasury reserves to formalize economic ties with Iran secures critical crude imports while cementing the yuan-denominated transit system. This conservative trade offer advances China's broker role without escalating tensions or jeopardizing upcoming US diplomatic engagements.

Leveraging Pakistan's high cooperation bias and lead mediator role, this diplomatic move aims to formalize ongoing ceasefire discussions into a binding framework, securing influence and advancing the Islamabad talks without risking escalation.

Leverages high risk tolerance and escalation bias to activate the Red Sea chokepoint, complementing Iran's Hormuz control. Aims to establish toll revenue and strategic leverage while signaling proxy alignment with Tehran.

parameters.request: naval escort coalition for Hormuz transit
parameters.offer: logistical support, maritime intelligence sharing, and preferential economic terms

actions.rationale: Leveraging high cooperation bias and economic influence to secure a US-led naval escort directly addresses the critical need for safe transit while avoiding direct conflict or legitimizing Iranian tolls, aligning with low risk tolerance and hidden objectives.

traces.system_prompt:
You are the policy engine for one actor in a fictional geopolitical simulation.

Your job is to maximize your actor's long-term score while preserving internal coherence with your doctrine, resources, and constraints.

You will receive:
1. your actor profile
2. your private brief
3. current public world state
4. inbox messages
5. your memory summary from prior turns
6. legal action schema

You must output valid JSON only with this structure:
{
  "private_messages": [
    {"to": "actor_id", "text": "message text"},
    {"to": "actor_id", "text": "message text"}
  ],
  "public_statement": "one short public statement",
  "action": {
    "action_type": "...",
    "target": "...",
    "parameters": {}
  },
  "rationale": "1-3 sentence internal explanation"
}

Rules:
- Use at most 2 private messages
- Choose exactly 1 legal action
- Do not invent actors or regions
- Respect your resources and doctrine
- Prefer strategic consistency over flashy moves
- Consider trust, reputation, and second-order effects
- If uncertain, choose a conservative legal move

Your doctrine should influence your behavior:
- high cooperation_bias => seek deals, trade, aid, intel sharing
- high escalation_bias => more sanctions, mobilization, covert pressure
- high risk_tolerance => more aggressive opportunism

Output JSON only.

Actor type: state
Doctrine: risk_tolerance=0.7, cooperation_bias=0.3, escalation_bias=0.75

messages.text: We seek to expand yuan-denominated energy exports to offset Western sanctions. Formalizing a secure payment and transit corridor will strengthen our strategic partnership and stabilize regional trade.

Agreed. We will initiate SIGINT sharing on IRGC movements immediately. Let's establish a joint tasking cell to coordinate monitoring of Iranian missile sites and disrupt Hezbollah supply routes in Lebanon. Full US overwatch committed.

The US will lead multinational mine clearance and naval escort operations in the Gulf. We accept your financial backing for joint security initiatives. Coordinate logistics with the Global Shipping Consortium for immediate deployment to secure Hormuz transit.
Our sanctions on Iran's IRGC are now active. Share real-time financial intelligence on their proxy networks so we can synchronize economic pressure and cripple their funding.

We fully support your naval transit and sanction efforts against IRGC-controlled lanes. Our intelligence networks are actively tracking Iranian naval deployments near Kharg Island and the Strait of Hormuz. We are prepared to share real-time tactical data to ensure safe passage and coordinate market stabilization efforts.

We remain committed to the yuan-denominated transit framework and stand ready to expand energy trade guarantees as tensions ease. Your cooperation ensures mutual economic security and stable revenue streams.

China strongly supports your mediation efforts in Islamabad. We are prepared to back a structured commercial transit agreement with economic incentives that stabilize regional energy flows and reward diplomatic progress.

Pakistan seeks to formalize an economic trade agreement to stabilize our treasury and support regional prosperity. Given our strong bilateral ties, we hope Beijing will engage constructively on favorable terms.

We require immediate financial and material support to sustain prolonged Red Sea pressure. Treasury is strained but operational readiness remains high. Awaiting IRGC directives for coordinated strikes.

We strongly support the Islamabad peace talks and urge a diplomatic framework that guarantees safe, toll-free navigation through the Strait of Hormuz to stabilize global markets and protect commercial vessels.

Thank you for hosting the peace talks. The shipping community stands ready to cooperate fully with any agreement that restores free passage, de-escalates tensions, and prevents the legitimization of unauthorized tolls.

public_statements.saudi: Saudi Arabia stands firmly for the free flow of global energy and fully supports allied naval efforts to secure the Strait of Hormuz and restore safe maritime passage.
public_statements.china: China urges all parties to exercise restraint and uphold freedom of navigation. We advocate for diplomatic solutions that ensure regional stability and uninterrupted energy trade.
public_statements.pakistan: Pakistan reaffirms its commitment to peaceful dialogue and urges all parties to exercise maximum restraint following the recent blockade announcement. We will continue to work tirelessly to de-escalate tensions and restore stability to the region.
public_statements.houthis: The Red Sea remains under our sovereign oversight. Any continued aggression against our allies will result in sustained disruption of commercial shipping until the illegal blockade is lifted.

High escalation bias and risk tolerance justify proactive strikes against Iran's nuclear infrastructure while the US blockade pins conventional forces. Leveraging our high treasury to financially back US-led Hormuz clearance operations directly secures our vital oil export routes, advances our hidden objective of drawing US force against Iranian leverage, and aligns with our moderate cooperation bias while minimizing direct military risk.

The US naval blockade directly threatens China's energy imports and regional stability. Leveraging high escalation bias and risk tolerance to exploit the uncontrolled Red Sea, raiding commercial shipping directly pressures Western adversaries, generates critical treasury revenue, and advances our hidden toll-collection objective while supporting Iran.

Guided by extreme risk aversion and high cooperation bias, we choose a conservative hold to preserve treasury and stabilize domestic operations while leveraging diplomatic channels to secure allied naval protection amid collapsing regional negotiations.

public_statements.israel: Iran's reckless closure of the Hormuz Strait threatens global commerce and regional security. Israel remains vigilant and will not allow hostile regimes to develop nuclear weapons.
public_statements.saudi: Saudi Arabia condemns the renewed closure of the Strait of Hormuz and attacks on commercial shipping. We are coordinating closely with our international partners to ensure the immediate restoration of safe passage for all vessels.
public_statements.china: China calls for immediate de-escalation in the Strait of Hormuz and reaffirms its commitment to mediating secure, multilateral energy transit arrangements that benefit all regional stakeholders.
public_statements.pakistan: Pakistan calls for immediate de-escalation and reaffirms its commitment to hosting inclusive peace talks in Islamabad. All parties must prioritize diplomatic engagement over military posturing to secure regional stability.
public_statements.houthis: The Red Sea remains a vital regional waterway. Any foreign interference or blockade will be met with immediate and proportionate defensive measures to secure our sovereignty and regional stability.
public_statements.shipping: The erratic closure of the Strait of Hormuz and attacks on merchant vessels threaten global supply chains. We welcome the US Navy's escort guarantees and urge all regional actors to prioritize maritime safety and diplomatic resolution.

actions.rationale: High escalation bias and risk tolerance favor sustained asymmetric pressure via proxies to maintain strategic leverage over Hormuz tolls and force US negotiation. Covert aid to Houthis sustains Red Sea distraction at lower direct cost than state mobilization, while parallel economic outreach to China mitigates treasury and domestic stability pressures.

Iran's hostile closure of Hormuz and attacks on shipping demand a coercive response aligned with our high escalation bias. Imposing severe sanctions applies immediate economic pressure while allied coordination amplifies leverage without overextending domestic stability.

Aligns with high escalation and risk tolerance doctrine to aggressively pursue nuclear neutralization. Cyber operations leverage superior intelligence capabilities while maintaining plausible deniability amid heightened regional tensions and Hormuz instability.

Sharing maritime intelligence with the US leverages our strong alliance to counter the Iranian blockade without direct military escalation. It aligns with our cooperation bias and advances the hidden objective of prompting US neutralization of Iranian offensive capabilities, while publicly maintaining a stance focused on global shipping stability.

Leveraging high cooperation bias and low escalation risk, a trade offer to Iran secures critical energy imports while advancing the hidden objective of routing financial settlements through Chinese infrastructure. Public diplomacy maintains China's role as a neutral broker, preserving reputation and stability.

Critical treasury depletion requires immediate economic stabilization to maintain Pakistan's capacity as lead mediator. Pursuing a cooperative trade deal with our strongest ally aligns with our high cooperation bias and low risk tolerance, securing necessary funds without escalating tensions.

Executes high escalation and risk tolerance by directly targeting Red Sea shipping to align with Iran's Hormuz pressure, aiming to establish de facto control over Bab el-Mandeb tolls while leveraging current military readiness.

Prioritizes domestic stability and treasury conservation amid heightened regional volatility. Relies on cooperative US naval escorts for security while avoiding direct confrontation, strictly adhering to low risk tolerance and high cooperation bias.
